person.name: Keshav Dial
person.headline: Doctor.Builder.Entrepreneur
person.bio: PhD Biochemist turned AI engineer and founder. Builds AI systems at the intersection of biology, agriculture, and unconventional problem domains. Strong background in machine learning, biomedical knowledge systems, and transforming legacy processes with modern AI.

entities.name: EndoBio AI
entities.description: AI-native agricultural intelligence platform detecting biological crop signals before visible disease symptoms appear. EndoBio combines molecular sensing, edge AI, and knowledge graphs to provide farmers with early warning systems, enabling intervention 7-14 days before traditional visual symptom detection. The agriculture market is $1.3T globally with massive crop losses to disease annually.
entities.summary: An AI-native agricultural intelligence platform that detects biological crop signals before visible symptoms appear.
entities.problem_statement: Current agricultural disease detection happens too late. By the time farmers see visible symptoms, infection is established and difficult to treat. Plants emit molecular, chemical, and biological signals 7-14 days before visible symptoms manifest. Farmers lose billions annually to preventable crop diseases.
entities.vision: Enable farmers to detect and respond to disease, stress, and nutrient deficiencies at the molecular level, transforming agriculture from reactive to predictive. Become the early-warning system for global food security.
entities.unfair_advantages: Unique combination of PhD-level biochemistry expertise with full-stack AI/ML engineering. Built production biomedical knowledge systems at Sanofi. Edge AI architecture eliminates connectivity requirements. Knowledge graph integration enables continuous learning across growing dataset.
entities.market_opportunity: Global agriculture: $1.3T market. Crop losses from disease: $220B annually. Precision agriculture: $20B market growing 15% CAGR.

entities.name: Sanofi
entities.description: Global pharmaceutical company. Keshav worked in R&D AI/ML, building production-grade AI systems including the Sanofi Concierge (internal agentic system), biomedical knowledge graph platform, and LLM applications for document synthesis and enterprise knowledge retrieval.

entities.name: McMaster University
entities.description: Canadian research university in Hamilton, Ontario where Keshav earned his PhD in Biochemistry.

entities.name: PhD in Biochemistry
entities.description: Doctoral research at McMaster University focused on biochemical systems and molecular processes. Thesis available at: https://macsphere.mcmaster.ca/handle/11375/28495

entities.name: Machine Learning & AI Systems
entities.description: Production-grade experience building end-to-end ML systems from data pipeline to deployment. Expertise in LLM applications, fine-tuning models on domain data, building retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and creating reusable ML infrastructure. Experience with model evaluation, MLflow, and production monitoring.

entities.name: Biomedical Knowledge Graphs
entities.description: Architected large-scale biomedical knowledge graph platforms integrating public databases (GeneCards, MalaCards, STRING) with proprietary data. Built graph-based RAG systems and natural language querying interfaces before mainstream Graph RAG adoption. Experience with Neo4j and semantic relationships in biomedical domains.

entities.name: Biomedical AI Engineering
entities.description: Combines deep biochemistry knowledge with practical AI implementation. Experience building domain-specific models, fine-tuning on internal biomedical datasets, cross-species pharmacodynamic modeling, and creating reusable tested packages for biomedical compute. Understands both the science and the engineering.

entities.name: Full-Stack System Design
entities.description: End-to-end system architecture spanning frontend, backend, ML, cloud infrastructure. Designed document-to-text pipelines, serverless architectures, infrastructure-as-code approaches, and cloud-native deployment. Experience with React, Docker, Lambda, S3, Terraform, and enterprise-scale observability.

entities.name: Biotech & Biology
entities.description: PhD-level training in biochemistry combined with practical biotech experience. Understands molecular systems, signal transduction, disease mechanisms, pharmacodynamics, and how biological processes work at scale. Applies this knowledge to AI problems in agriculture, pharmaceuticals, and diagnostics.

entities.name: Sanofi Concierge
entities.description: Internal AI agent system for Sanofi R&D and operations. Solo-built POC that was subsequently deployed globally. Handles process automation, knowledge retrieval, and decision support across pharmaceutical R&D. Demonstrates ability to take AI from concept to global enterprise deployment.

entities.name: AgTech & Unconventional AI Integration
entities.description: Passionate about applying AI to industries where it's not yet conventional. Agriculture represents a massive, underexplored domain for AI. Beyond farming, interested in integrating AI across drug manufacturing, personalized medicine, and other areas where biology meets scale.

entities.name: Breaking Conventional Paradigms
entities.description: Driven by the idea that we're in a tech revolution. Believes in rethinking how we approach established industries and processes. Not interested in incremental improvements, but transformative applications of technology.

entities.name: Beauty
entities.summary: Keshav's dog, featured on the homepage.

memories.title: Why EndoBio exists
memories.text: Keshav founded EndoBio because agriculture detects disease too late. Plants emit molecular, chemical, and biological signals 7-14 days before visible symptoms appear. The core insight: by the time farmers see problems, they're too late to intervene effectively. EndoBio's mission is to shift agriculture from reactive to predictive by integrating AI, molecular sensing, and edge computing to detect signals invisible to human observation.

memories.title: What Keshav is building now
memories.text: Keshav is currently focused on building EndoBio AI, combining molecular sensing hardware, edge AI inference, and knowledge graphs to create an early-warning system for agricultural disease and stress. He's working on integrating unconventional signals (biological, chemical, electrical) that plants emit before symptoms manifest, applying the same agentic and ML techniques he built at Sanofi.

memories.title: Building Sanofi Concierge from zero
memories.text: While at Sanofi, Keshav solo-built the proof-of-concept for Sanofi Concierge, an internal agentic system that ended up deployed globally across the company. This was not a small project—it demonstrates the full journey from idea to enterprise-scale rollout: architecture, development, testing, security review, and global deployment. The Concierge handles R&D process automation, knowledge retrieval, and decision support.

memories.title: Pioneering biomedical knowledge graphs at Sanofi
memories.text: Keshav architected an integrated biomedical knowledge graph platform at Sanofi that pulled together public databases (GeneCards, MalaCards, STRING) with proprietary drug discovery data in Neo4j. He built custom tooling and early large-scale graph-based RAG with natural-language querying—this was before Graph RAG became mainstream hype. The platform was foundational to multiple R&D teams' discovery work.

memories.title: PhD in Biochemistry from McMaster
memories.text: Keshav earned his PhD in Biochemistry from McMaster University in Hamilton, Ontario. His doctoral research focused on biochemical systems and molecular processes, providing the deep foundational knowledge that informs his approach to both biomedical AI and agricultural biotechnology. The thesis work demonstrates rigorous training in experimental design and molecular understanding.

memories.title: Full-stack AI systems engineer
memories.text: Keshav is not just an ML researcher or a frontend engineer—he builds complete end-to-end AI systems. At Sanofi, he designed and built systems spanning frontend (React), backend, ML models, cloud infrastructure (AWS Lambda, S3, Docker, Terraform), and observability (Datadog). This full-stack capability is rare and critical for shipping real products.

memories.title: PhD biochemist turned AI engineer
memories.text: Keshav's unusual background—deep biochemistry training (PhD) combined with practical AI engineering (Sanofi, EndoBio)—gives him a unique perspective. Most AI engineers don't understand biology deeply. Most biologists don't build production systems. This combination makes him well-suited to tackle problems at the intersection of biology and technology.

memories.title: Passion for applying AI where it's unconventional
memories.text: Keshav has implemented AI and agentic solutions across diverse industries: drug manufacturing, agriculture, personalized medicine, and more. He's interested in breaking conventional paradigms—not applying AI to problems that already have AI solutions, but to areas where AI hasn't yet been applied at scale. Agriculture is a prime example: massive global industry, rich biological data, but AI-native approaches are rare.

memories.title: Evolution at Sanofi from Data Engineer to Product Engineer
memories.text: Keshav's role at Sanofi evolved from Data & Machine Learning Engineer to R&D AI/ML Product Engineer (May 2023 - Sep 2025, 2+ years). This progression reflects movement from building individual systems to thinking about product strategy, user needs, and enterprise deployment. He moved from 'how do we build this?' to 'what should we build and how does it fit into the organization?'

memories.title: Breakfast preference
memories.text: Keshav once had waffles and chicken with BBQ and maple syrup for breakfast—and apparently enjoyed it enough to mention it.

memories.title: Has two fridges in the kitchen
memories.text: Keshav has two fridges in his kitchen. Practical or passion for cold storage—unclear, but it's a thing.

memories.title: Why agriculture is the right market for AI
memories.text: Agriculture is a $1.3 trillion global market where AI adoption is still nascent. Farmers lose $220 billion annually to preventable crop diseases. Most agriculture still relies on visual inspection and reactive treatment. This is exactly where AI can have immediate, measurable impact. Unlike software-first SaaS, EndoBio solves a hardware + software problem that creates defensibility.

memories.title: Unfair advantage: Biochemistry + Full-Stack AI Engineering
memories.text: Most AI founders don't understand biology deeply. Most biologists can't build production systems. Keshav's unfair advantage is the rare combination: PhD-level biochemistry training plus hands-on experience building enterprise ML systems at Sanofi (knowledge graphs, LLM applications, edge AI). This means EndoBio's technology isn't just theoretical—it's grounded in how biology actually works and built to production standards from day one.

memories.title: Edge AI eliminates connectivity constraints
memories.text: EndoBio's edge AI architecture is critical for farming environments with poor or no connectivity. The system can run on-device, requiring only occasional data sync. This solves the connectivity problem that kills most AgTech solutions and creates a moat: farmers don't need expensive infrastructure, just sensors and local compute.

memories.title: 7-14 day early detection window
memories.text: Plants emit detectable molecular, chemical, and biological signals 7-14 days before visible disease symptoms appear. This window is critical: it's enough time for farmers to intervene with preventive treatment before infection is established. By the time symptoms are visible, it's too late for most fungicides and management strategies.

memories.title: Enterprise AI deployment experience
memories.text: At Sanofi, Keshav solo-built the Concierge POC and saw it rolled out globally—not as a research project, but as a mission-critical internal system. This demonstrates ability to: navigate enterprise requirements, get security/compliance approval, achieve adoption, and iterate based on real-world feedback. This skill transfers directly to getting EndoBio adopted by farm operations.
